location: Dropped by the NPC invader Swordhand of Night Anna, who ambushes the player on the path to the hanging bell inside the Finger Ruins of Miyr — a hidden chamber reached via a secret ladder behind Count Ymir's throne in the Cathedral of Manus Metyr.
tactics: Interact with Count Ymir's throne while he is not sitting on it to reveal a rope ladder down to Finger Ruins of Miyr — this only opens after progressing Ymir's questline (ring the bells at Finger Ruins of Rhia and Finger Ruins of Dheo first).

Anna invades partway along the path to the hanging bell, before you reach the bell that summons the area boss Metyr, Mother of Fingers — don't confuse the invasion fight with the Metyr boss fight further ahead.
She is fast and low-poise: closing in immediately and chaining quick attacks keeps her staggered and unable to counter; hanging back lets her punish you with her own claw strings.
Watch your stamina during aggressive strings so you still have enough to dodge or block if she breaks free of hitstun.
lore: These claws are an inky, light-swallowing black, and were the personal weapon of Anna, the Swordhand of Night. Smithscript work on the blades lets a charged attack fling all three claws outward as returning projectiles, and the blades themselves are only half-real, letting them cut through an opponent's guard.

builds.name: Dex Bleed Claws
builds.why: Scales primarily with Dexterity (C, improvable to B) and carries high blood loss buildup (45), rewarding a fast dual-claw bleed build that strings hits together.

builds.name: Ranged Poke Harasser
builds.why: The innate Scattershot Throw skill launches all three claws as zero-FP projectiles that return to hand, letting a dex build zone and punish from range between melee exchanges.
